Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Brought By William Chow, Representative For Diana Tom'S Estate
Off Calendar May-31-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Soriano, Esq., Tel. 415.673.5600, appearing via Zoom for plaitntiff Samir Hinn;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, Esq., Tel. 415.987.7000, appearing in person for William Chow Representative for Diana Tom's estate.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT WILLIAM CHOW'S Motion For Attorney's Fees Brought By William Chow, Representative For Diana Tom'S Estate.

The matter is contested. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted with one modification. The Court adds back in the amount of $624.00 following Mr. Volkov's explanation that the date set forth in his declaration was a mistake. The date of 01/25/21 was incorrect. The correct date for the entry is 01/25/22. The Court finds the fees of $624.00 appropriate since it was after the 998. Counsel is to add the $624.00 and submit another cost bill.

Representative William Chow's motion for attorney's fees is granted in part.

At the May 3, 2024, hearing counsel were directed to provide a new cost bill, in light of the court's finding that Diana Tom was the prevailing party, but some items should be disallowed.

First, the court specified that fees were appropriate from January 21, 2022, forward, the date the 998 offers were served on plaintiffs. Accordingly, the 1/25/21 fee is disallowed. (Mem. of Costs 5:2.)

Second, the court indicated that Tom's counsel should have known efforts to block the trustee's settlement would be unsuccessful, as Tom lacked decision-making authority. The fees relating to Tom's counsel's efforts to oppose the settlement; appeal the determination of a good faith settlement; and settle the appeal are disallowed. (Entries dated 4/22/23 through 5/26/23; 6/26/23; 7/3/23 through 9/20/23; 10/26/23.)

Third, per CCP 1032(b), a prevailing party is entitled as a matter of right to recover costs. Plaintiff cites no statute to the contrary. Accordingly, all costs listed in the amended bill are allowed. (Mem. of Costs 4:1-25.)

Fourth, the court did not authorize a sur-reply, so associated costs and fees are disallowed.

Fifth, without more information, it is impossible for the court discern whether the 4/7/23 entry pertained to the trustee's settlement, so it is disallowed.

Representative Chow's counsel shall file an updated cost bill per this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The proposed order is to comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

Atty. BRIAN SORIANO, Esq. Goldstein, Gellman, Melbosstad, Harris & McSparran, LLP (tel: 415-673-5600) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. ALEKSANDR VOLKOV, Esq. of Volkov Law Firm, Inc. (tel: 415-987-7000) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT WILLIAM CHOW's Motion For Attorney'S Fees Brought By William Chow, Representative For Diana Tom'S Estate.

Matter discussed. Argued and submitted. Upon conclusion of oral argument the Court orders as follows:

The Court does not find that Ms. Tom was bound by the settlement agreement. The Court finds the 998 offer is a brightline and Plaintiff should have known Ms. Tom was not a proper defendant and she had no connection with the decisions concerning the property or the lease. The Court finds attorneys fees are appropriate from January 21, 2022 onward. The Court notes there were claims under tort in the underlying dispute and fees are permitted when there is a tort case. The Court finds some fees associated with the property would not be part of the fees for which plaintiffs are responsible. The Court would tax any costs associated with dispute between Ms. Tom in her individual capacity and Ms. Hodges as the trustee.
The Court grants in part the motion for attorney's fees.
Counsel is to provide the Court with a new costs bill by May 22, 2024. Any responses are due the same date.
The matter is continued to May 31, 2024 at 9:30 A.M. in Department 302.
